mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series 428i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 428i engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with the B48 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. A blown engine replacement on a BMW can cost between £9,000 and £13,000. Used engines can be found from £800–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 4 Series.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 4 Series 428i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine era your 428i has. The pre-LCI N20 (2013–2017) has a well-documented timing chain guide failure risk — plastic guides can disintegrate, causing catastrophic engine failure. The LCI B48 (2017–2020) is a significant improvement, with the timing chain issues resolved. The N20 also suffers from oil leaks and water pump issues. A post-2017 B48-engined car is the safer choice.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series 428i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issues are timing chain guide failure on the N20 (pre-LCI) — plastic guides become brittle and break, oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing, water pump failure (particularly on the N20, with a fire risk recall), and occasional turbocharger wastegate rattle. The B48 (LCI) is significantly more reliable.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 428i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept F32/F33/F36 worth £8,000–£15,000, a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 25–60% of the car's value — often worth it for a cherished example. Against a tired pre-LCI car with significant body issues, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B48-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series 428i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-LCI F32/F33/F36 (2013–2017) 428i engines carry the N20B20A code. LCI F32/F33/F36 (2017–2020) use the B48B20 code. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation. If your car is registered after mid-2017, it's almost certainly the B48.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The chain guides must be replaced — they are the weak point. A preventative replacement of the timing chain and guides costs £1,500–£3,000. A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £3,500–£5,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 428i? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-LCI (2017+) with the B48 engine (significantly more reliable)
* Pre-LCI (2013–2017) with documented timing chain guide replacement
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
* No cold-start rattling (listen carefully on first start)
* No oil leaks around the oil filter housing or valve cover
avoidIf: * Pre-LCI N20 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N20 is unforgiving of neglect
* Oil leaks around the oil filter housing (belt damage risk)
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — may indicate water pump or head gasket issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 428i is BMW's entry-level 2.0-litre turbocharged petrol in the first-generation 4 Series. The N20 (pre-LCI, 2013–2017) is a capable engine let down by one critical design flaw — timing chain guide failure. The B48 (LCI, 2017–2020) is a significant improvement, resolving the chain issues and offering greater reliability. With typical F32/F33/F36 values ranging from £8,000 to £20,000+, a £4,000–£5,500 engine replacement is a significant investment. A post-2017 B48-engined car is the safer choice.

cta.label: → Compare 428i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 4 Series 428i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (N20 timing chain guide warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20 or B48) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain — For N20 (pre-LCI): check for any chain work or cold-start rattle
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing for leaks
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate water pump issues
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
